title: Technology Stack | Logos
description: Modular by design. Storage, Messaging, Blockchain, and User Modules form the foundation for privacy-first decentralised applications.
heading: The Logos Technology Stack

sections.eyebrow: Open by design, open in spirit. Build truly decentralised applications with the modular Logos Technology Stack.
sections.headline: The Logos Technology Stack
sections.body: The Logos technology stack is a unified ecosystem of distinct modules. The whole stack is modular, allowing developers to assemble their own Logos-based platform from their own selection of modules.

sections.title: The Technology Stack
sections.eyebrow: Disclaimer: Abstract representation of the stack.

architecture.title: Architecture Overview
body: Logos gives developers a modular, plugin-based runtime for building decentralised, privacy-preserving applications.

Nodes discover and load modules dynamically while preserving privacy at every layer. The network’s architecture lets it evolve over time, providing a foundation for decentralised applications and institutions that remain resistant to capture and corruption – whether they are run by local communities or nation states.

sections.networkingTitle:
The Networking Stack:
Discovery, Peering, and Mix-Net
sections.networkingDescription: This layer handles how Logos nodes find each other, establish connections, and communicate.
sections.foundationDescription: A microkernel that handles the essential primitives every decentralised application needs.

sections.title: Logos Basecamp
sections.description: Basecamp is the launcher and unified surface of the Logos stack. It is an executable that wraps the Logos runtime, initialises the Logos Core environment, and discovers and loads plugins from installed modules.

body: Most blockchain platforms provide consensus only — you bring your own messaging and storage, or rely on centralised fallbacks. Logos ships all three as a unified stack with privacy at every layer.

Bitcoin proved that decentralised value transactions are possible. Ethereum generalised this for arbitrary computation. Logos extends it further: a platform for deploying parallel institutions — governance systems, communication networks, data archives — with network-level and transaction-level privacy.

Where Zcash focuses on private transactions, Logos provides a full application platform. Where Polkadot focuses on chain interoperability, Logos focuses on privacy and self-governance. Where Cosmos provides a framework for sovereign chains, Logos provides a complete integrated stack.

sections.title: Open Source
sections.subtitle: All Logos code is open source, licensed under permissive licences. The primary development happens across several GitHub organisations.

rows.title: logos-co
rows.description: Basecamp, module system, protocol modules, LEZ, SDKs, dev tooling

sections.heading: Use Cases
sections.subheading: We know today’s systems are captured and brittle. We build alternatives to give people a choice.
sections.subheadingExtra: Build on Logos and serve your community.
